name: Llanuras de Torozos — circular desde Vezdemarbán
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campos-Pan offers diverse terrain, from river valleys along the Duero and Valderaduey to forested areas like Valorio Forest. The region features a mix of mostly gentle rolling hills and flatter sections, with some routes including moderate elevation gains. Riders can explore historical paths and natural reserves, providing varied landscapes for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Campos-Pan The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta del Duero and GR 14: Zamora to Pereruela Loop, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route follows the Duero river, offering scenic views and a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valorio Forest from Zamora - circular route via Zamora Cathedral and Olivares Mills, an easy 14.1 miles (22.7 km) path. This trail leads through the Valorio Forest, combining natural beauty with historical landmarks. Local mountain bikers also love the Vía de la Plata: Castrotorafe Castle Loop, a 39.5 miles (63.6 km) trail leading through historical landscapes and open countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Campos-Pan is defined by river valleys, forests, and natural reserves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through wetlands to moderate trails with significant elevation changes. The routes in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campos-Pan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Campos-Pan?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike trails around Campos-Pan, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 30 easy routes, 90 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Campos-Pan?
A: The terrain around Campos-Pan is diverse, featuring river valleys along the Duero and Valderaduey, as well as forested areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest&lt;/a&gt;. You'll find a mix of mostly gentle rolling hills and flatter sections, with some routes including moderate to significant elevation gains, especially on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, Campos-Pan offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306662113/valorio-forest-from-zamora-circular-route-via-zamora-cathedral-and-olivares-mills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest from Zamora - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.7 km long and takes about 1 hour 39 minutes. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018301714/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-from-palacios-del-pan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.2 km route.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking in Campos-Pan?
A: Many trails in Campos-Pan pass by interesting landmarks. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Zamora&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Campos-Pan are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306773684/ruta-del-duero-and-gr-14-zamora-to-pereruela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Duero and GR 14: Zamora to Pereruela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306662113/valorio-forest-from-zamora-circular-route-via-zamora-cathedral-and-olivares-mills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest from Zamora - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309691194/via-de-la-plata-castrotorafe-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata: Castrotorafe Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Campos-Pan?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to forests, and the combination of natural beauty with historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, making it appealing to many.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails in Campos-Pan that pass by water features like rivers or lakes?
A: Absolutely. Several trails follow river valleys or pass by reservoirs and lagoons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306773684/ruta-del-duero-and-gr-14-zamora-to-pereruela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Duero and GR 14: Zamora to Pereruela Loop&lt;/a&gt; follows the Duero river, offering scenic views. You can also explore routes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; within the Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are suitable for advanced riders looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate or easy, there are challenging options for advanced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a moderate route spanning 52.9 km with significant elevation gain, providing a good test for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Campos-Pan?
A: The region around Campos-Pan generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While specific weather details vary, the diverse terrain means there are options to enjoy in most seasons, though summer can be hot and winter might bring colder conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that combine natural beauty with historical elements?
A: Many trails in Campos-Pan offer a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306662113/valorio-forest-from-zamora-circular-route-via-zamora-cathedral-and-olivares-mills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest from Zamora - circular route&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the forest and past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309691194/via-de-la-plata-castrotorafe-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata: Castrotorafe Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; leads through historical landscapes and open countryside, following parts of an ancient Roman road.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or bird observatories along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy scenic views and wildlife, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otero de Sariegos Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the region. Trails around the Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018926811/reserva-natural-de-lagunas-de-villafafila-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de Lagunas de Villafáfila — ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, also offer opportunities for birdwatching and enjoying the natural landscape.
tips: Vezdemarbán is a typical flat town. It has a beautiful natural environment although it is mostly dry cereal fields around. The city center is a mixture of old and modern buildings. The most interesting is the church of Santa María de la Cuesta and San Miguel Arcángel.
Located next to the Valderaduey River, Castronuevo de los Arcos is a small town in the province of Zamora. The river is one of the tributaries of the Duero River.
This stretch of road is really precious. There is not much traffic and it connects Castronuevo with Cañizo. It is especially pleasant in spring.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de la Cuesta and Saint Michael the Archangel
CL-612 Road Between Castronuevo and Cañizo
